name: Spanish Hard Rolls (Bolillos)
recipeIngredient: 2 pkg. active dry yeast (I use rapid-rise)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine yeast and salt.
2. Add 5 cups of flour and mix until blended.
3. Stir in honey and 2 cups of warm water on low speed until the mixture is thick.
4. Switch to dough hook and knead for about 7 minutes, adding the remaining flour in half-cup increments during the first 4 minutes of kneading.
5. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turning the greased side up. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
6. Punch down the dough; cover and let rise for an additional 15-20 minutes.
7. Divide the dough into 16 equal pieces. Shape each piece into an oblong about 5 inches long and pinch the ends to form points.
8. Place the rolls on greased baking sheets. Make a slash about 3 inches long and 1/2-inch deep along the length of each roll.
9. Cover and let rise until doubled, 40-60 minutes.
10. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
11. Mix together cornstarch and 2-1/2 tsp water; brush gently on rolls.
12. Bake until the rolls are golden brown, about 35-40 minutes. Serve warm.
NOTE: These rolls freeze very well. Makes: 16 rolls.
